blocks.content: She made it until after dinner.
Her mom was washing dishes when Daisy came into the kitchen and sat down at the table without a book or homework or a reason.
Her mom dried her hands and sat down across from her and didn't say anything.
She hadn't meant to cry. It came up without warning and she pressed her fingers to the bridge of her nose and breathed through it.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I don't even have a reason to be upset.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: We're not together. He didn't do anything wrong.
Her mom reached across the table and covered her hand, and Daisy stopped talking, and for a minute the kitchen was just quiet.

blocks.speaker: Mrs. Jones
blocks.content: You've been carrying this a long time.
Daisy looked at her mom. Her mom looked back, steady and certain, the way she looked when she wasn't going to unsay something.
